mn61:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the Squirrels’ Feeding Place.
mn61:10.1: While you are doing a bodily *kamma*, Rāhula, then that bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:10.2: ‘Does this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I am doing lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:10.3: is this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:10.4: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:10.5: ‘This bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I am doing does lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:10.6: this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] is unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then you should stop such a bodily *kamma*, Rāhula.
mn61:10.7: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:10.8: ‘This bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I am doing leads neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others, nor to the affliction of both—
mn61:10.9: this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] is skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then you should continue such a bodily *kamma*, Rāhula.
mn61:11.1: After you have done a bodily *kamma*, Rāhula, then that bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:11.10: this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] was skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then, Rāhula, you should dwell with *pīti* and joy, training by day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn61:11.2: ‘Did this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I did lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:11.3: was this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:11.4: If, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:11.5: ‘This bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I did led to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:11.6: this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] was unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then such a bodily *kamma* should be confessed, disclosed, and laid open by you, Rāhula, to the Teacher or to the wise among your spiritual companions.
mn61:11.7: Having confessed it, disclosed it, and laid it open, you should undertake restraint in the future.
mn61:11.8: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:11.9: ‘This bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I did led neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others, nor to the affliction of both—
mn61:12.1: “Whenever you want to do a verbal *kamma*, Rāhula, then that verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:12.2: ‘Would this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I want to do lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:12.3: is this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:12.4: If, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:12.5: ‘This verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I want to do would lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:12.6: this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] is unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then such a verbal *kamma* should certainly not be done by you, Rāhula.
mn61:12.7: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:12.8: ‘This verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I want to do would lead neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others—
mn61:12.9: this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] is skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then such a verbal *kamma* should be done by you, Rāhula.
mn61:13.1: While you are doing a verbal *kamma*, Rāhula, then that verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:13.2: ‘Does this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I am doing lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:13.3: is this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:13.4: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:13.5: ‘This verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I am doing does lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:13.6: this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] is unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then you should stop such a verbal *kamma*, Rāhula.
mn61:13.7: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:13.8: ‘This verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I am doing leads neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others, nor to the affliction of both—
mn61:13.9: this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] is skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then you should continue such a verbal *kamma*, Rāhula.
mn61:14.1: After you have done a verbal *kamma*, Rāhula, then that verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:14.10: this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] was skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then, Rāhula, you should dwell with *pīti* and joy, training by day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn61:14.2: ‘Did this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I did lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:14.3: was this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:14.4: If, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:14.5: ‘This verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I did led to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:14.6: this verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] was unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then such a verbal *kamma* should be confessed, disclosed, and laid open by you, Rāhula, to the Teacher or to the wise among your spiritual companions.
mn61:14.7: Having confessed it, disclosed it, and laid it open, you should undertake restraint in the future.
mn61:14.8: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:14.9: ‘This verbal *kamma* [*vacīkammaṁ*] that I did led neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others, nor to the affliction of both—
mn61:15.1: Whenever you want to do a mental *kamma*, Rāhula, then that mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:15.2: ‘Would this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I want to do lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:15.3: is this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:15.4: If, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:15.5: ‘This mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I want to do would lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:15.6: this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] is unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then such a mental *kamma* should certainly not be done by you, Rāhula.
mn61:15.7: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:15.8: ‘This mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I want to do would lead neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others, nor to the affliction of both—
mn61:15.9: this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] is skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then such a mental *kamma* should be done by you, Rāhula.
mn61:16.1: While you are doing a mental *kamma*, Rāhula, then that mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:16.2: ‘Does this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I am doing lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:16.3: is this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:16.4: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:16.5: ‘This mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I am doing does lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:16.6: this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] is unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then you should stop such a mental *kamma*, Rāhula.
mn61:16.7: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:16.8: ‘This mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I am doing leads neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others, nor to the affliction of both—
mn61:16.9: this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] is skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then you should continue such a mental *kamma*, Rāhula.
mn61:17.1: After you have done a mental *kamma*, Rāhula, then that mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:17.10: this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] was skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then, Rāhula, you should dwell with *pīti* and joy, training by day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn61:17.2: ‘Did this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I did lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:17.3: was this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:17.4: If, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:17.5: ‘This mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I did led to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:17.6: this mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] was unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then, Rāhula, you should be appalled by such a mental *kamma*, ashamed of it, and repelled by it.
mn61:17.7: Having been appalled, ashamed, and repelled, you should undertake restraint in the future.
mn61:17.8: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:17.9: ‘This mental *kamma* [*manokammaṁ*] that I did led neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others, nor to the affliction of both—
mn61:18.1: For whatever ascetics or brahmins in the past purified bodily *kamma*, purified verbal *kamma*, purified mental *kamma*—all of them purified bodily *kamma* in just this same way, by reflecting again and again; they purified verbal *kamma* by reflecting again and again; they purified mental *kamma* by reflecting again and again.
mn61:18.2: And whatever ascetics or brahmins in the future will purify bodily *kamma*, will purify verbal *kamma*, will purify mental *kamma*—all of them will purify bodily *kamma* in just this same way, by reflecting again and again; they will purify verbal *kamma* by reflecting again and again; they will purify mental *kamma* by reflecting again and again.”
mn61:18.3: And whatever ascetics or brahmins at present purify bodily *kamma*, purify verbal *kamma*, purify mental *kamma*—all of them purify bodily *kamma* in just this same way, by reflecting again and again; they purify verbal *kamma* by reflecting again and again; they purify mental *kamma* by reflecting again and again.
mn61:18.4: Therefore, Rāhula, you should train thus: ‘By reflecting again and again I will purify bodily *kamma*; by reflecting again and again I will purify verbal *kamma*; by reflecting again and again I will purify mental *kamma*’—
mn61:18.5: that is how you should train, Rāhula.”
mn61:18.7: Satisfied, the Venerable Rāhula delighted in what the *Bhagavā* said.
mn61:18.8: The first, Advice to Rāhula at Ambalaṭṭhikā, is finished.
mn61:2.1: Now at that time the Venerable Rāhula was dwelling at Ambalaṭṭhikā.
mn61:2.2: Then the *Bhagavā*, in the evening, having come out of seclusion [*paṭisallānā*], went to Ambalaṭṭhikā, to the Venerable Rāhula.
mn61:2.3: The Venerable Rāhula saw the *Bhagavā* coming in the distance.
mn61:2.4: Seeing him, he prepared a seat and water for the feet.
mn61:2.5: The *Bhagavā* sat down on the seat prepared.
mn61:2.6: Having sat down, he washed his feet.
mn61:2.7: Then the Venerable Rāhula paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side.
mn61:3.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having left a little remnant of water in the water vessel, addressed the Venerable Rāhula:
mn61:3.2: “Do you see, Rāhula, this little remnant of water left in the water vessel?”
mn61:3.4: “In the same way, Rāhula, slight is the contemplative life of those who feel no shame at lying with full awareness [*sampajāna*].”
mn61:4.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having thrown away that little remnant of water, addressed the Venerable Rāhula:
mn61:4.2: “Do you see, Rāhula, that little remnant of water thrown away?”
mn61:4.4: “In the same way, Rāhula, thrown away is the contemplative life of those who feel no shame at lying with full awareness [*sampajāna*].”
mn61:5.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having turned that water vessel upside down, addressed the Venerable Rāhula:
mn61:5.2: “Do you see, Rāhula, this water vessel turned upside down?”
mn61:5.4: “In the same way, Rāhula, overturned is the contemplative life of those who feel no shame at lying with full awareness [*sampajāna*].”
mn61:6.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having turned that water vessel upright, addressed the Venerable Rāhula:
mn61:6.2: “Do you see, Rāhula, this water vessel empty and hollow?”
mn61:6.4: “In the same way, Rāhula, empty and hollow is the contemplative life of those who feel no shame at lying with full awareness [*sampajāna*].
mn61:7.1: Suppose, Rāhula, a king’s elephant, a tusker, battle-trained, of great stature, well-bred, gone into battle, works with his forefeet, works with his hindfeet, works with the forepart of his body, works with the hindpart of his body, works with his head, works with his ears, works with his tusks, works with his tail; but he guards his trunk.
mn61:7.10: Therefore, Rāhula, you should train thus: ‘I will not speak falsely even in jest’—
mn61:7.11: that is how you should train, Rāhula.
mn61:7.3: ‘This king’s elephant, this tusker, battle-trained, of great stature, well-bred, gone into battle, works with his forefeet, works with his hindfeet … works with his tail; but he guards his trunk.
mn61:7.4: The king’s elephant has not yet given up his life.’
mn61:7.5: But when, Rāhula, that king’s elephant, that tusker, battle-trained, of great stature, well-bred, gone into battle, works with his forefeet, works with his hindfeet … works with his tail, and also works with his trunk, then the elephant-rider would think:
mn61:7.6: ‘This king’s elephant, this tusker, battle-trained, of great stature, well-bred, gone into battle, works with his forefeet, works with his hindfeet, works with the forepart of his body, works with the hindpart of his body, works with his head, works with his ears, works with his tusks, works with his tail, and also works with his trunk.
mn61:7.7: The king’s elephant has given up his life.
mn61:7.8: Now there is nothing at all that the king’s elephant would not do.’
mn61:7.9: In the same way, Rāhula, when someone feels no shame at lying with full awareness [*sampajāna*], I say there is no evil they would not do.
mn61:8.4: “In the same way, Rāhula, only after reflecting again and again should a bodily *kamma* be done, only after reflecting again and again should a verbal *kamma* be done, only after reflecting again and again should a mental *kamma* be done.
mn61:9.1: Whenever you want to do a bodily *kamma*, Rāhula, then that bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] should be reflected on by you:
mn61:9.2: ‘Would this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I want to do lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:9.3: is this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*?’
mn61:9.4: If, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:9.5: ‘This bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I want to do would lead to my own affliction, or to the affliction of others, or to the affliction of both—
mn61:9.6: this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] is unskillful, leading to *dukkha*, ripening in *dukkha*,’ then such a bodily *kamma* should certainly not be done by you, Rāhula.
mn61:9.7: But if, while reflecting, Rāhula, you should know thus:
mn61:9.8: ‘This bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] that I want to do would lead neither to my own affliction, nor to the affliction of others, nor to the affliction of both—
mn61:9.9: this bodily *kamma* [*kāyakammaṁ*] is skillful, leading to *sukha*, ripening in *sukha*,’ then such a bodily *kamma* should be done by you, Rāhula.
